2026-04-09T21:29:22.5785810Z ```json
2026-04-09T21:29:22.5785985Z {
2026-04-09T21:29:22.5786151Z   "create_issue": {
2026-04-09T21:29:22.5828383Z     "repository": "Azure/azure-sdk-for-net",
2026-04-09T21:29:22.5829104Z     "title": "[Transcription] Docs: README missing multilingual transcription example and preview notice",
2026-04-09T21:29:37.5617701Z     "body": "## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/`\n**Triggered by:** commit 6d10a352b (#58040) by @jsquire\n\n## What Changed\n\nThe push introduced the `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription` package (including all sample test files) and specifically marked `TranscribeWithPhraseList` tests as `[Ignore]` due to flakiness. The package ships with nine sample test files, each containing `#region Snippet:` markers, and the README references those snippets via `` ```C# Snippet: `` placeholders — **except** for the multilingual transcription feature (`Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs`).\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Missing Examples section entry:** The README `## Examples` table-of-contents (and body) covers 9 features but omits multilingual transcription. There is no `### Transcribe multilingual audio` heading or example code block anywhere in the README body.\n- **Orphaned snippet:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/tests/Samples/Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs` defines `#region Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` / `#endregion`, but no matching `` ```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel `` placeholder exists in the README. The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script therefore never injects this code into the README.\n- **No preview notice:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/samples/Sample_9_MultilingualTranscription.md` explicitly labels the feature *\"Multilingual Transcription (Preview)\"* and notes that the multilingual model outputs a \"major locale\" (e.g., always `en-US` for English). Neither the preview status nor the major-locale behavior is documented in the README's **Key concepts** or **Examples** sections.\n- **Supported locales not listed:** The standalone sample doc lists 14 supported locales (`de-DE, en-AU, en-CA, en-GB, en-IN, en-US, es-ES, es-MX, fr-CA, fr-FR, it-IT, ja-JP, ko-KR, zh-CN`); the README does not.\n\n<details>\n<summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/README.md` — **edit**\n\n1. Add `- [Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)](#transcribe-multilingual-audio-preview)` to the Examples table-of-contents after the \"Transcribe with a known language\" bullet (around line 150).\n\n2. Add the following new section to the **Examples** body, after the existing `### Transcribe with a known language` section and before `### Use Enhanced Mode for highest accuracy`:\n\n````markdown\n### Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)\n\nWhen your audio contains speech that switches between languages, you can use the multilingual transcription model by **not specifying any locales**. The service automatically detects and transcribes each language segment.\n\n> [!NOTE]\n> Multilingual transcription is currently in **preview**. The model reports a \"major locale\" per phrase (for example, always `en-US` for English regardless of accent). Supported locales: `de-DE`, `en-AU`, `en-CA`, `en-GB`, `en-IN`, `en-US`, `es-ES`, `es-MX`, `fr-CA`, `fr-FR`, `it-IT`, `ja-JP`, `ko-KR`, `zh-CN`. For more information, see [Fast transcription – Multilingual transcription](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/speech-service/fast-transcription-create?tabs=multilingual-transcription-on).\n\n```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel\n```\n````\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nThe snippet `TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` already exists in the test file. No new test code is needed.\n\n- **Test file:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/tests/Samples/Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs`\n  - The `#region Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` / `#endregion` is already present; no changes needed.\n- **README location:** Insert the placeholder `` ```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel `` (followed by a closing `` ``` ``) in the new `### Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)` section added in Step 1.\n- **Snippet name:** `TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` — must match exactly.\n\nAfter inserting the placeholder, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 transcription` to inject the code automatically.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README already contains all required sections in the correct order:\n\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\n2. ✅ Key concepts\n3. ⚠️ Examples — **missing** the multilingual transcription entry (fix in Step 1)\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\n5. ✅ Next steps\n6. ✅ Contributing\n\nNo other structural changes are needed.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order from the repository root. Each must succeed before proceeding:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 transcription` — injects `Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` into the README. Verify with `git diff sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/README.md` that the code block was populated.\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 transcription` — only needed if public API surface changed; no public API changes are expected for this doc-only update.\n5. `dotnet format sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/` — ensures consistent code formatting.\n\n</details>\n\n## Next Steps\n\n> [!TIP]\n> **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above"

Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-09T21:33:24.7139953Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T21:32:42.456026588Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/`\n**Triggered by:** commit 6d10a352b (#58040) by @jsquire\n\n## What Changed\n\nThe push introduced the `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription` package (including all sample test files) and specifically marked `TranscribeWithPhraseList` tests as `[Ignore]` due to flakiness. The package ships with nine sample test files each containing `#region Snippet:` markers, and the README references those snippets via `` ```C# Snippet: `` placeholders — **except** for the multilingual transcription feature (`Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs`).\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Missing Examples section entry:** The README `## Examples` table-of-contents (and body) covers 9 features but omits multilingual transcription. There is no `### Transcribe multilingual audio` heading or example code block anywhere in the README body.\n- **Orphaned snippet:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/tests/Samples/Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs` defines `#region Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` / `#endregion`, but no matching `` ```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel `` placeholder exists in the README. The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script therefore never injects this code into the README.\n- **No preview notice:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/samples/Sample_9_MultilingualTranscription.md` explicitly labels the feature _\"Multilingual Transcription (Preview)\"_ and notes that the multilingual model outputs a \"major locale\" (e.g., always `en-US` for English regardless of accent). Neither the preview status nor the major-locale behavior is documented in the README's **Key concepts** or **Examples** sections.\n- **Supported locales not listed:** The standalone sample doc lists 14 supported locales (`de-DE, en-AU, en-CA, en-GB, en-IN, en-US, es-ES, es-MX, fr-CA, fr-FR, it-IT, ja-JP, ko-KR, zh-CN`); the README does not.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/README.md` — **edit**\n\n1. Add `- [Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)](#transcribe-multilingual-audio-preview)` to the Examples table-of-contents after the \"Transcribe with a known language\" bullet (around line 150).\n\n2. Add the following new section to the **Examples** body, after the existing `### Transcribe with a known language` section and before `### Use Enhanced Mode for highest accuracy`:\n\n````markdown\n### Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)\n\nWhen your audio contains speech that switches between languages, you can use the multilingual transcription model by **not specifying any locales**. The service automatically detects and transcribes each language segment.\n\n\u003e [!NOTE]\n\u003e Multilingual transcription is currently in **preview**. The model reports a \"major locale\" per phrase (for example, always `en-US` for English regardless of accent). Supported locales: `de-DE`, `en-AU`, `en-CA`, `en-GB`, `en-IN`, `en-US`, `es-ES`, `es-MX`, `fr-CA`, `fr-FR`, `it-IT`, `ja-JP`, `ko-KR`, `zh-CN`. For more information, see [Fast transcription - Multilingual transcription](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/ai-services/speech-service/fast-transcription-create?tabs=multilingual-transcription-on).\n\n```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel\n```\n````\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nThe snippet `TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` already exists in the test file. No new test code is needed.\n\n- **Test file:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/tests/Samples/Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs`\n  - The `#region Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` / `#endregion` is already present; no changes needed.\n- **README location:** Insert the placeholder after the preview note in the new `### Transcribe multilingual audio (preview)` section added in Step 1.\n- **Snippet name:** `TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` — must match exactly.\n\nAfter inserting the placeholder, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 transcription` to inject the code automatically.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README already contains all required sections in the correct order:\n\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\n2. ✅ Key concepts\n3. ⚠️ Examples — **missing** the multilingual transcription entry (fix in Step 1)\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\n5. ✅ Next steps\n6. ✅ Contributing\n\nNo other structural changes are needed.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order from the repository root. Each must succeed before proceeding:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 transcription` — injects `Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` into the README. Verify with `git diff sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/README.md` that the code block was populated.\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 transcription` — only needed if public API surface changed; no public API changes are expected for this doc-only update.\n5. `dotnet format sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/` — ensures consistent code formatting.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above","integrity":"high","secrecy":"public","temporary_id":"aw_transcDoc1","title":"[Transcription] Docs: README missing multilingual transcription example and preview notice"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-09T21:33:24.7459821Z 
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7459943Z **Package:** `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription`
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7460148Z **Service directory:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/`
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7460277Z **Triggered by:** commit 6d10a352b (#58040) by @jsquire
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7460281Z 
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7460353Z ## What Changed
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7460357Z 
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7462087Z The push introduced the `Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription` package (including all sample test files) and specifically marked `TranscribeWithPhraseList` tests as `[Ignore]` due to flakiness. The package ships with nine sample test files each containing `#region Snippet:` markers, and the README references those snippets via `` ```C# Snippet: `` placeholders — **except** for the multilingual transcription feature (`Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs`).
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7462092Z 
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7462163Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7462167Z 
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7462983Z - **Missing Examples section entry:** The README `## Examples` table-of-contents (and body) covers 9 features but omits multilingual transcription. There is no `### Transcribe multilingual audio` heading or example code block anywhere in the README body.
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7464421Z - **Orphaned snippet:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/tests/Samples/Sample09_MultilingualTranscription.cs` defines `#region Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel` / `#endregion`, but no matching `` ```C# Snippet:TranscribeWithMultilingualModel `` placeholder exists in the README. The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script therefore never injects this code into the README.
2026-04-09T21:33:24.7465827Z - **No preview notice:** `sdk/transcription/Azure.AI.Speech.Transcription/samples/Sample_9_MultilingualTranscription.md` explicitly labels the feature _"Multilingual Transcription (Preview)"_ and notes that the multilingual model outputs a "major locale" (e.g., always `en-US` for English regardless of accent). Neither the preview status nor the major-locale behavior is documented in the README's **Key concepts** or **Examples** sections.

2026-04-09T21:33:24.7465981Z ...
